summary: FURROW_ROOT resolves to the source repo even when the worktree has a newer schema
detail: bin/frw line 9: FURROW_ROOT is derived from the frw binary's location. In a worktree that symlinks bin/frw back at the source repo, frw validate-definition reads the source's schema, not the worktree's. Had to validate manually (python3 + jsonschema). This is exactly the self-hosting ambiguity install-architecture-overhaul targets — it bit us inside the ideation of its own fix.

summary: Worktree schema edits don't propagate to running tooling (FURROW_ROOT resolution)
detail: rws/frw on PATH symlink to /home/jonco/src/furrow/bin/*. Their FURROW_ROOT resolution uses readlink -f on the script path, ignoring env overrides. So any schema edit made in a worktree is invisible to validate-definition. To unblock ideation of install-and-merge itself, had to mirror the schemas/definition.schema.json source_todos addition into the source repo outside the worktree — crossing exactly the boundary this row is designed to enforce. install-architecture-overhaul must account for this resolution path or the worktree <-> source drift will keep biting.

summary: Parallel Explore subagents are efficient for codebase-only research topics
detail: R1/R2/R3 dispatched in parallel covered install-mutation inventory, common.sh split, and boundary-evidence respectively — all pure codebase analysis. Three agent contexts returned in one round with concrete path:line citations each. R4 (web/prior-art) and R5 (design) done in main thread after the codebase facts returned — proper sequencing because R5 depended on R1-R3.

summary: common.sh split line is 168; exactly 7 hook-safe functions
detail: R2 confirmed all 12 hooks use only: log_warning, log_error, find_active_row, find_focused_row, read_state_field, row_name, extract_row_from_path (+ is_row_file in one). No hook uses replace_md_section, set_focus, clear_focus, read_definition_field, or gate helpers. Evidence: hook-by-hook symbol matrix in r2-commonsh-split.md.

summary: User prefers chronologically-visible sort for almanac files
detail: Given options A (id-only), B (id+created_at), C (created_at+id), user chose C. Reason: when reading seeds.jsonl or todos.yaml with less/cat, chronological ordering is more useful than id-sorted. Consumers using jq/yq don't care about file order.

summary: Plan reviewer feedback converts to concrete plan.json sub-steps, not prose revisions
detail: Fresh reviewer flagged 3 concerns: launch-phase ownership overlap, drift check not wired, --no-verify test missing. Each became a concrete addition — AD-8/AD-9 plus plan.json sub-steps 1m/1n with scope_notes. Cross-model codex flagged 'research-grounding' — fixed by adding explicit §section citations to each AD. This is a reliable pattern: review findings → structured plan additions, not free-form revisions.

summary: cross-model-review --spec writes to a non-deliverable-specific file
detail: bin/frw.d/scripts/cross-model-review.sh writes spec reviews to .furrow/rows/{name}/reviews/spec-cross.json regardless of which deliverable was passed. Running it 4 times in a row for 4 different deliverables overwrites the same file; only the last review survives. This is a harness bug — should write to spec-{deliverable}-cross.json. Workaround this session: read each review from the per-task background output files.

summary: Parallel spec-agent dispatch is efficient but produces cross-spec consistency bugs
detail: 4 parallel sub-agents each wrote a spec independently. Without cross-talk, they diverged on shared contracts: migration_version type, common-minimal.sh function count, exit code semantics, CLI flag existence. Dual review caught 13 concrete consistency issues. The fix pattern is: after parallel spec-writing, dedicate a consolidation pass for cross-spec invariants (shared JSON shapes, CLI contracts, reference targets). Would have been cheaper to run that consolidation proactively rather than via review.
